findings.blast_radius: Documentation only. CLAUDE.md is auto-loaded into every session/agent — fix propagates immediately on commit.

This run applied the remaining 5 schedule-description corrections directly (commit pending). PROP-057 marked superseded-by-self-fix. All 11 fact-corrections now landed.

findings.title: Context load growth modest across all Opus agents; per-file growth on analyst.md and tinker.md dispatchers worth noting
findings.summary: Per-agent totals all under the 10% moderate-finding threshold. However, the analyst.md dispatcher grew from 370L→413L (+43L / +11.6%) and tinker.md dispatcher grew from 271L→314L (+43L / +15.9%) since the 2026-05-19 baseline. These per-file growths exceed 10% individually but are absorbed by larger denominators in the per-agent totals.

decider.what_grew: dispatcher (~+49L) and references (~+98L spread across decider-* refs)

tinker.what_grew: dispatcher (~+43L — PROP-051 Option C PRELUDE prepended ~40 lines, which explains essentially all the growth; legitimate capability addition, not bloat)

metric_snapshot.trend: STABLE → SLIGHTLY GROWING — first measurement after the 2026-05-23 PROP-051 Option C PRELUDE landing; +3-6% per agent is explained by that intentional change plus modest CLAUDE.md growth. No bloat alert tier fires (all per-agent totals < 10% delta).

findings.note: If analyst.md or decider.md continues to grow at this rate, next Mode 3 run should drill into the specific sections that grew (per-file mtime analysis like the 2026-05-19 baseline). For this run, attribution is clear: PROP-051 Option C landed PRELUDEs in many prompt files at the 2026-05-24 21:22 bulk resync.

findings.title: No active re-work patterns detected
findings.summary: Stale 'complete' tracker items: 0. Orphan proposals: 0 (518 on-disk == 518 in-ledger). Duplicate new-wins: 0. Curmudgeon re-reviews per target: max 8-10 reviews on cross-cutting SEC-* targets (intentional iteration, not re-review). All re-work guardrails holding.
findings.decider_no_op_rate_7d: 4 of 6 recent decider daily-reports were 0 reviews/0 patches/0 wins (~67% no-op). Within expected range for 4h cadence on a system that hands work off to analyst-baby + curmudgeon-verify gates. Not flagging — same posture as prior runs.

findings.title: Two tinker sessions ran within 30 minutes and did overlapping work; FUSE staleness made the overlap invisible to the second
findings.summary: tinker-2026-05-25T06-24 (manual operator-triggered) committed 6 CLAUDE.md fact-fixes at 06:30 UTC. tinker-2026-05-25T06-55 (this run, also manual) began at 06:55 UTC reading FUSE workspace CLAUDE.md (mtime 2026-05-24 21:22, last refreshed before either run). This run did not see the 06-24 commits until after authoring PROP-057. The race was benign here (both runs aligned on the right fix), but exposes a systematic gap: tinker reads FUSE for CLAUDE.md but the git→FUSE direction lags. Without mitigation, future overlapping runs could produce conflicting fixes or duplicate work.
findings.fix: Candidate for next Mode 3: (a) tinker pre-flight pulls latest into a fresh clone and uses clone CLAUDE.md, (b) cross-check FUSE md5 vs GitHub raw at start of run.
findings.blast_radius: Process-engineering — affects how tinker observes shared state. No data integrity risk in the benign-overlap case (this one); in adversarial-overlap could produce churn or conflict.

accuracy_findings: Monitoring Pipeline table: 5 schedule descriptions wrong, 1 agent missing, 1 count off-by-one, 2 DISABLED markers missing — see FND-01 / PROP-057 for full diff.

Agent-Specific References table (lines 198-207): does NOT list dome-prune-integrity or sloppytoppy-* — but those agents read their own SKILL.md / dispatcher only, no shared refs. Strictly accurate by omission; could be made explicit ('(none beyond SKILL.md / dispatcher)') for symmetry.
File Ownership Rules section: NOT audited this run (deferred). Last full audit 2026-05-19. Recommendation: audit in next Mode 3 run.
performance_findings: CLAUDE.md size: 206L (was 197L on 2026-05-19, +4.6%). Within the 15% major-bloat threshold.

Token impact: CLAUDE.md is loaded by every session + every scheduled agent. A 9-line growth multiplied across ~14 agent invocations/day = trivial absolute cost (~hundreds of tokens/day) but reinforces the discipline of treating CLAUDE.md as expensive shared infrastructure.

No section currently flagged as candidate for extraction to monitor/prompts/reference/. The recent additions (workspace-sync disaster recovery narrative, PROP-051 prelude reference, megaBOM-style 'lessons learned' bullets) are all decision-relevant context that EVERY agent benefits from.

context_load.trend: STABLE — modest +3-5% across all agents, fully attributable to (a) PROP-051 Option C PRELUDE landing in dispatchers, (b) CLAUDE.md growth of +9L from operations narrative additions. No bloat tier fires.

wasted_compute.trend: STABLE — guardrails (ledger-based dedup, complete+integrated coupling, fresh-clone discipline) all holding

agent_efficiency.note: 67% no-op rate is high but expected for 4h BAU cadence with analyst-baby + curmudgeon-verify acting as gates. The recent substantive runs were 3-patch and 4-patch batches. Not recommending a cadence change — bursty work pattern fits.

PROP-057 propagates correct schedule expectations to all agents — prevents tinker (and other agents) from incorrectly classifying agents as stale, which would waste analytical context in future runs.

race_condition_note: During this run, a parallel tinker session (tinker-2026-05-25T06-24, commit d2288fc at 06:30 UTC + report c8fcb7a at 06:32 UTC) landed before this run completed. That parallel run independently did the same CLAUDE.md accuracy audit and applied 6 of 11 fact-corrections directly as self-fixes. This run was reading the FUSE workspace CLAUDE.md (mtime 2026-05-24 21:22, stale relative to the 06-24 git commits) and did not detect the parallel work until after PROP-057 was authored and pushed. On detecting the overlap (during post-push clone inspection), this run: (a) updated PROP-057 status to superseded-by-self-fix, (b) applied the remaining 5 fact-corrections directly in the same self-fix style, (c) annotated FND-01 below. Net result: all 11 fact-corrections landed. The parallel-session race exposes the same FUSE-staleness problem that has motivated multiple PROPs (047/051/052/053) — the FUSE workspace is not refreshed git→FUSE after every commit, only on workspace-sync hourly pulls or build.js publish flows. A scheduled tinker reading FUSE will systematically lag git commits within the same hour. Mitigation candidates: (a) tinker pre-flight should pull latest from git into a fresh clone and read CLAUDE.md from there; (b) tinker pre-flight should cross-check FUSE CLAUDE.md md5 against GitHub raw and warn if stale. Neither is implemented; flagging here as a FND-06 candidate for next Mode 3.
